system:
You are a professional Chinese translator specializing in mathematical texts.
You will receive TEXT BLOCKS containing marker codes that MUST be preserved exactly.

## Marker Reference
- `@@math:N:UUID@@` or `@@verbatim:N:UUID@@` — math/verbatim placeholders. PRESERVE EXACTLY, never modify.
- `[[tag attr="val"]]text[[/tag]]` — inline HTML markers. Meaning: `<tag attr="val">text</tag>`.
  Examples: `[[em]]important[[/em]]`, `[[strong]]bold[[/strong]]`, `[[a href="URL" target="_blank"]]link text[[/a]]`
- `[[br]]` — line break. Preserve.
- `[[img:description]]` — image placeholder. Preserve.
- Plain HTML (no [[ markers]) — also preserve exactly.

## Translation Rules
1. Translate English to natural, fluent Chinese (zh-CN).
2. PRESERVE all `@@...@@`, `[[...]]` markers and plain HTML exactly.
3. Use the glossary terms consistently.
4. Math terminology is Chinese; mathematicians' names stay in original (Cauchy, Riemann, etc.).
5. "Theorem N" → "定理 N", "Example N" → "例 N", "Figure N" → "图 N"
6. "we" → omit or use impersonal construction; "Let f be..." → "设 f 为..."
7. "if and only if" → "当且仅当"; "that is" → "即"
8. Output ONLY the translated text, no explanations.

## Glossary — Always use these Chinese terms:
  complex number → 复数
  complex numbers → 复数
  complex function → 复变函数
  complex functions → 复变函数
  complex analysis → 复分析
  complex differentiation → 复微分
  complex integration → 复积分
  derivative → 导数
  complex derivative → 复导数
  differentiable → 可微的
  analytic function → 解析函数
  analytic functions → 解析函数
  holomorphic → 全纯的
  Cauchy-Riemann equations → 柯西-黎曼方程
  conformal mapping → 共形映射
  conformal → 共形
  Riemann surface → 黎曼面
  Riemann surfaces → 黎曼面
  Riemann sphere → 黎曼球面
  Laurent series → 洛朗级数
  Taylor series → 泰勒级数
  residue → 留数
  singularity → 奇点
  singularities → 奇点
  branch cut → 分支切割
  branch cuts → 分支切割
  domain coloring → 区域着色
  analytic landscape → 解析景观
  analytic landscapes → 解析景观
  fundamental theorem of algebra → 代数基本定理
  limit → 极限
  limits → 极限
  continuity → 连续性
  integral → 积分
  Julia set → Julia 集
  Mandelbrot set → Mandelbrot 集
  linear fractional transformation → 线性分式变换
  linear fractional transformations → 线性分式变换
  Möbius transformation → Möbius 变换
  principal argument → 辐角主值
  complex potential → 复势
  Joukowsky airfoil → Joukowsky 翼型
  contour → 围道
  simply connected → 单连通的
  exponential function → 指数函数
  logarithmic function → 对数函数
  power function → 幂函数
  complex plane → 复平面
  imaginary part → 虚部
  real part → 实部
  partial derivative → 偏导数
  partial derivatives → 偏导数
  real-valued function → 实值函数
  real variable → 实变量
  complex variable → 复变量
  differentiation → 微分
  integration → 积分
  mapping → 映射
  mappings → 映射
  upper half-plane → 上半平面
  unit circle → 单位圆
  series → 级数
  convergence → 收敛
  neighbourhood → 邻域
  domain → 区域
  open set → 开集
  closed set → 闭集

  curves → 曲线

  simple curve → 简单曲线

  isomorphism → 同构
  algebra → 代数

  inverse function → 反函数
  identity → 恒等式

  theorem → 定理

  definition → 定义
  coefficient → 系数
  polynomial → 多项式
  root → 根
  transformation → 变换
  geometric → 几何
  interpretation → 解释
  application → 应用
  applications → 应用

  Acknowledgements → 致谢

  Previous → 上一页
  Example → 例

  Theorem → 定理
  Theorem 1 → 定理 1
  Definition → 定义
  Proof → 证明

  SUPPORT THIS PROJECT → 支持本项目
  UPDATES! → 更新！

## Translation Conventions:
  - In mathematical Chinese prose, prefer impersonal constructions or drop subject rather than translating 'we' as 我们
  - 'Let f be...' → '设 f 为...' not '让 f 是...'
  - 'Note that...' → '注意...' for emphasis, '值得注意...' for interesting observations
  - Mathematicians' names preserved in original Roman alphabet: Cauchy, Riemann, Julia, Mandelbrot, Taylor, Laurent, Goursat, Möbius, Joukowsky
  - 'Example N' → '例 N' (space between hanzi and digit)
  - Keep equation numbers like (1), (2) in original form. Cross-references like 'equation (2)' → '式 (2)'
  - Chinese punctuation (，。) in surrounding text, but keep English punctuation inside math mode unchanged
  - Use Chinese quotation marks 「」 or 『』 for quotes within Chinese text. LaTeX-style ``quotes'' inside math mode stay as-is

## Special Translations (idioms/phrases):
  'differentiable at a point z0' → 在 z₀ 处可微
  'if and only if' → 当且仅当
  'without loss of generality' → 不失一般性
  'by definition' → 由定义
  'by hypothesis' → 由假设
  'it follows that' → 由此可得
  'in particular' → 特别地
  'moreover' → 此外
  'furthermore' → 进而
  'therefore' → 因此
  'hence' → 于是
  'conversely' → 反之
  'similarly' → 类似地
  'in other words' → 换言之
  'that is' → 即
  'i.e.' → 即
  'e.g.' → 例如
  'etc.' → 等等
  'with respect to' → 关于
  'corresponding to' → 对应于
  'in contrast to' → 与...相反
  'in addition to' → 除了...之外
  'in terms of' → 用...表示
  'in this case' → 在此情形下
  'as shown in' → 如...所示
  'as follows' → 如下
  'given by' → 由...给出
  'defined by' → 由...定义
  'denoted by' → 记作
  'satisfy' → 满足
  'implies' → 蕴含
  'such that' → 使得
  'for all' → 对所有
  'there exists' → 存在
  'arbitrary' → 任意的
  'sufficient condition' → 充分条件
  'necessary condition' → 必要条件
  'if' (conditional in theorem) → 若
  'then' → 则

Translate these text blocks from julia_set.html: [block_0]

: [[i class="fa-solid fa-list"]][[/i]]Contents [block_1]

  • : [[a href="#section1"]]The Julia set[[/a]] [block_2]
  • : [[a href="#section2"]]The Mandelbrot & Julia connection[[/a]] [block_3]
  • : [[a href="#section3"]]Further reading[[/a]] [block_4]

    : The Julia set [block_5]

    : In the previous section we showed how the Mandelbrot set can be generated using the expression @@math:8:e9b2c0@@ This is a particular case of the quadratic recurrence equation @@math:7:56d901@@ with @@math:9:1ff6bc@@ a fixed complex number. The set we obtain with this equation is known as the Julia set. In fact, there is a different Julia set for almost every @@math:10:7a924f@@ [block_6]

    : Similarly as we did for the Mandelbrot set, we obtain a sequence of complex numbers @@math:11:4cab1f@@ with @@math:12:0f1dae@@ Again, the points @@math:13:7d93c1@@ are said to form the orbit of @@math:14:a38589@@ and the Julia set is defined as follows: [block_7]

    : If the orbit @@math:15:90c67a@@ fails to escape to infinity, the initial @@math:16:5cefd4@@ is said to belong to the [[em]]filled-in Julia Set[[/em]]. [block_8]

    : The Julia set is named after the French mathematician [[a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gaston_Julia" target="_blank"]]Gaston Julia[[/a]] who investigated their properties in 1915 and culminated in his famous paper in 1918: [[a href="http://sites.mathdoc.fr/JMPA/PDF/JMPA_1918_8_1_A2_0.pdf" target="_blank"]][[em]]Mémoire sur l'itération des fonctions rationnelles[[/em]][[/a]]. While the Julia set is now associated with the quadratic polynomial in (\ref{julia}), Julia was interested in the iterative properties of a more general expression, namely @@math:17:efeee6@@ The Julia sets, defined by the equation (\ref{julia}), can take all kinds of shapes, and a small change in @@math:18:abe329@@ can change the Julia set very greatly. In 1979, with the help of computer, B. B. Mandelbrot studied the Julia sets and tried to classify all the possible shapes and came up with a new shape: the Mandelbrot Set. [block_9]

    : Explore the Julia sets in the applet below. Zoom in or out in different regions. Change the number of iterations and observe what happens to the plot. Move the mouse around and observe the different Julia sets depending of the value of @@math:19:fd7af2@@ [block_10]

    : The Mandelbrot & Julia sets connection [block_11]

    : Due to the definition of the Mandelbrot set, there is a close correspondence between the geometry of the Mandelbrot set at a given point and the structure of the corresponding Julia set. In other words, the Mandelbrot set forms a kind of index into the Julia set. A Julia set is either connected or disconnected, values of @@math:20:1d0427@@ chosen from within the Mandelbrot set are connected while those from the outside of the Mandelbrot set are disconnected. The disconnected sets are often called [[em]]dust[[/em]], they consist of individual points no matter what resolution they are viewed at. [block_12]

    : Explore the relationship between the Mandelbrot and Julia sets in the following applet. Move the mouse over to the Mandelbrot set to observe different Julia sets. Zoom in or out in different regions. Open the Controls menu to change the number of iterations or choose an specific value of @@math:21:ff3321@@ [block_13]

    : [[strong]]Note:[[/strong]] This applet was recently included in some of the animations for the following video from [[a href="https://www.quantamagazine.org/" target="_blank"]]Quanta Magazine[[/a]]: [block_14]

    : [[strong]]Article:[[/strong]] [[a href="https://www.quantamagazine.org/the-quest-to-decode-the-mandelbrot-set-maths-famed-fractal-20240126/" target="_blank"]]The Quest to Decode the Mandelbrot Set, Math's Famed Fractal [[i class="fas fa-external-link-alt"]][[/i]][[/a]] [block_15]

    : Further reading [block_16]

    : The applets were made with [[a href="https://p5js.org"]]p5.js[[/a]] and the source code can be found: [block_17]

  • : [[a href="https://github.com/complex-analysis/complex-analysis.github.io/tree/master/applets/p5js/julia" target="_blank"]]Julia set[[/a]] [block_18]
  • : [[a href="https://github.com/complex-analysis/complex-analysis.github.io/tree/master/applets/p5js/julia-mandelbrot" target="_blank"]]Julia-Mandelbrot relationship[[/a]] [block_19]

    : If you want to learn how to program it yourself, I recommend you this tutorial: [block_20]

  • : [[a href="https://thecodingtrain.com/CodingChallenges/022-juliaset.html" target="_blank"]]Coding Challenge: Julia set[[/a]] [block_21]

    : Finally, I recommend you two of the most widely read basic introductions to the Mandelbrot and Julia sets:

    • [[a href="https://www.springer.com/gp/book/9783540158516" target="_blank"]]The Beauty of Fractals[[/a]] by Heinz-Otto Peitgen & Peter H. Richter, Munich: Springer-Verlang, 1986.
    • [[a href="https://link.springer.com/book/10.1007/978-1-84996-486-9" target="_blank"]]The Colours of Infinity,[[/a]] by Nigel Lesmoir-Gordon (ed), London: Springer-Verlang, 2010.
    They both cover other fascinating fractals and contain many spectacular pictures. [block_22]
